steps.content: The graph passes through $(10, 0)$ and $(-6, 0)$, so those are the two zeros of $f$. Write $f(x) = a(x - 10)(x + 6)$ where $a$ is a positive integer greater than 1.
Expand: $f(x) = a(x^{2} - 4x - 60) = ax^{2} - 4ax - 60a$. Comparing with $ax^{2} + bx + c$, we get $b = -4a$.
Therefore $a + b = a + (-4a) = -3a$. Since $a$ is an integer greater than 1, $a \geq 2$, so $a + b \leq -6$.

Evaluating all choices:
  • "$6$": This requires $-3a = 6 \Rightarrow a = -2$, but $a$ must be a positive integer. Incorrect.
  • "$4$": This requires $-3a = 4 \Rightarrow a = -\frac{4}{3}$, but $a$ must be a positive integer. Incorrect.
  • "$-3$": This requires $-3a = -3 \Rightarrow a = 1$, but $a$ must be greater than 1. Incorrect.
  • "$-6$": This requires $-3a = -6 \Rightarrow a = 2$, which is an integer greater than 1 ✓. That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
